checkFormalGuestList.name: Check the Formal Guest List
checkFormalGuestList.description: Write a function that checks if a person is on the guest list by their surname. Given an honorific and surname (e.g., 'Mr Pitt'), check if any name on the list ends with that surname.

bonusMultiWordSurname.name: Multi-word Surnames
bonusMultiWordSurname.description: Handle surnames that have multiple words, like 'Lloyd Webber'.

emptyList.name: Empty register
emptyList.description: No-one's allowed in when the list is empty.

nameMissing.name: Brad's turned away
nameMissing.description: The name's not on the list.

namePresent.name: Brad's allowed in
namePresent.description: The name's on the list (Brad Pitt matches Mr Pitt).

differentHonorific.name: Knight of the realm
differentHonorific.description: Handle a different honorific (Lord Doyle matches Arthur Conan Doyle).

bondAllowed.name: Mr Bond, I presume
bondAllowed.description: James Bond is on the list.

bondNotAllowed.name: Hmmm.. Mr Bond, I presume
bondNotAllowed.description: Only Jason Bourne on the list, not James Bond.

lloydWebber.name: How about a Baron?
lloydWebber.description: Baron Lloyd Webber has two words in his surname.

markWebber.name: Is Mark the Baron?
markWebber.description: A different Webber doesn't count - surname must match fully.

extractSurname.question: How do I get just the surname from a full name?
extractSurname.answer: The honorific (Mr, Mrs, Dr, etc.) comes at the start, followed by a space, then the surname. If you can drop the part up to (and including) the first space, what's left is the surname.

matchGuest.question: How do I check if a guest matches that surname?
matchGuest.answer: Loop through the guest list. For each guest, check whether their full name ends with the surname you extracted. A helper like `endsWith()` makes this clean.

endsWithPitfall.question: Why might endsWith() give the wrong answer?
endsWithPitfall.answer: 'Smithson' ends with 'son', and 'Worthington' ends with 'ton'. You probably want to check that the guest ends with a space followed by the surname (e.g. ' Smith'), so partial matches don't trip you up.

helpersNeeded.question: What helpers will I need?
helpersNeeded.answer: Likely `length()` (to know how long things are) and `endsWith()` (for matching the surname). Build the space-prefixed comparison string using concatenation (`+`) or a template string.
